audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 2 Samuel chapter 20, first-pass draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Sheba's secession, ten concubines' lifelong confinement after rape, Amasa's deadline, Joab's deceptive greeting and murder, corpse exposure, coerced troop loyalty, siege of Abel, wise woman's negotiation, collective city risk, Sheba's beheading, and forced-labor administration.

verses.restored: A worthless/rebellious man was there, named Sheba son of Bichri, a Benjaminite. He blew shofar and said, We have no share in David and no inheritance in son of Jesse. Each to his tents, Israel.
Every man of Israel went up from after David and followed Sheba son of Bichri, but men of Judah clung to their king from Jordan to Jerusalem.

David came to his house at Jerusalem. King took ten women, concubines he left to guard house, placed them in guarded house, and sustained them, but did not come to them. They were confined until day of their death, living as widows.
King said to Amasa, Summon men of Judah for me within three days, and you stand here.
Amasa went to summon Judah, but delayed beyond appointed time set for him.
David said to Abishai, Now Sheba son of Bichri will do us more evil than Absalom. Take servants of your lord and pursue him, lest he find fortified cities and escape our eyes.
Men of Joab went out after him, with Cherethites, Pelethites, and all mighty warriors. They went out from Jerusalem to pursue Sheba son of Bichri.
They were near great stone at Gibeon when Amasa came before them. Joab wore military garment, and upon it a belt with sword fastened at his waist in its sheath. As he went forward it fell/came out.
Joab said to Amasa, Is it peace with you, my brother? Joab took Amasa by beard with his right hand to kiss him.

Amasa did not guard himself from sword in Joab's hand. Joab struck him in belly, spilling his inward parts to ground; he did not strike him again, and he died. Joab and Abishai his brother pursued Sheba son of Bichri.
One of Joab's young men stood beside him and said, Whoever delights in Joab and whoever is for David, after Joab.
Amasa was wallowing in blood in middle of highway. Man saw all people stood still. He moved Amasa from highway to field and threw garment over him when he saw everyone coming to him stood still.
When he was removed from highway, every man passed after Joab to pursue Sheba son of Bichri.
Sheba passed through all tribes of Israel to Abel, Beth-maacah, and all Berites. They gathered and also came after him.
They came and besieged him in Abel Beth-maacah. They poured up siege mound against city, standing at rampart, and all people with Joab were destroying wall to cause it to fall.
A wise woman called from city, Hear, hear. Please say to Joab, Come near here, that I may speak with you.
He came near to her. Woman said, Are you Joab? He said, I am. She said, Hear words of your maidservant. He said, I hear.
She spoke, saying, Speaking, they used to speak in former time, saying, Let them surely inquire at Abel; thus they completed matters.
I am among peaceful and faithful of Israel. You seek to put to death a city and a mother in Israel. Why will you swallow inheritance of YHWH?
Joab answered and said, Far be it, far be it from me, that I should swallow or destroy.

Matter is not so. A man from hill country of Ephraim, Sheba son of Bichri his name, lifted his hand against king, against David. Give him alone, and I will go from city. Woman said to Joab, Look, his head will be thrown to you over wall.
Woman came to all people in her wisdom. They cut off head of Sheba son of Bichri and threw it to Joab. He blew shofar; they scattered from city, each to his tent. Joab returned to Jerusalem to king.
Joab was over all army of Israel. Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over Cherethites and Pelethites.
Adoram was over forced labor/tribute, and Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder.
Sheva was scribe, and Zadok and Abiathar were priests.
Ira the Jairite was also priest/chief officer to David.

translation_notes: The ten concubines were raped publicly in chapter 16 after David left them behind. He now confines them for life, provides food, and withdraws sexual access. This is not survivor-centered care, freedom, justice, or chosen widowhood.
Their confinement protects royal honor and controls dynastic sexuality. The women receive no recorded hearing, compensation, household autonomy, or choice of future.
Joab greets Amasa as 'my brother,' takes his beard as if to kiss him, and murders him. Intimate gestures can be weaponized by violent officials.
Amasa's exposed body stops the army until it is moved and covered. The corpse is treated as an operational obstruction after command assassination.
The soldier's cry merges loyalty to Joab with loyalty to David, demonstrating how an unauthorized commander can capture institutional allegiance through violence.
Joab threatens an entire city for one fugitive. Siege warfare places civilians, food, homes, and a 'mother in Israel' under collective danger.
The wise woman saves the city through negotiation, but the outcome is summary beheading without a recorded trial. Collective survival under siege constrains meaningful consent.
Joab denies wanting destruction while actively battering the wall; stated intent must be tested against actual methods.
The administrative list includes forced labor/tribute. Restored royal order still rests upon coercive extraction.
safety_note: Do not call the ten women's lifelong confinement compassionate, excuse Joab's murder as efficiency, or praise Sheba's beheading as clean justice. Center survivors' autonomy, due process, civilian protection, and the wise woman's challenge: no ruler may swallow a whole community to reach one opponent.
